_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in writing code in a currently supported programming language
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature engineering, model training, and evaluation, including documentation and unit testing.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI AI domain. Skill AI-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-001 (TF, cosine: 0.527), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.458).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.7/10 (raw 0.573). Shared vocabulary: [code, skill, writing]. NICE-only terms [currently, language, programming, supported] vs WIDAI-only terms [data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature, model] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.876 (P=0.899, R=0.854). P exceeds R by 0.044 — the NICE element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). "Skill in writing code in a currently supported programming language" and "Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature eng" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.581 ≈ STS 0.573 (gap 0.008). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [code, skill, writing] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.0%. Shared tokens: [code, skill, writing]. NICE-only: [currently, language, programming, supported]. WIDAI-only: [data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature, model].
